title: poa eleanorae
intro: Poa eleanorae, also known as Eleanor's grass, is a rare and endemic plant species found in New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive yellow-green flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and as a diuretic. The health benefits of Poa eleanorae include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and provide relief from respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Maori medicine.
